findings.title: No threat model file found in .mcp/, docs/, or security/
evidence: Searched for: threat-model.json, threat-model.md under .mcp/, docs/, security/
requiredActions: Create a threat model document (threat-model.json or threat-model.md) in .mcp/, docs/, or security/.
Include STRIDE analysis, OWASP Top-10 mapping, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, trust boundaries, and data flow diagrams.
Reference the threat model from your PR description and link it to changed components.

findings.title: No SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) found in repository
evidence: Searched for CycloneDX (.cdx.json, bom.json, bom.xml) and SPDX (.spdx, .spdx.json) files.
requiredActions: Generate an SBOM in CycloneDX or SPDX format and commit it to the repository.
For Node.js: `npx @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm --output-file sbom.json`.
For Python: `cyclonedx-bom -o sbom.json`.
Automate SBOM generation in CI so it stays current with every dependency change.
Ensure the SBOM is included in any artifact upload to your registry (SLSA Level 2+).

findings.title: Compliance-related files changed but no compliance gap analysis found
evidence: Searched .mcp/, security/, and docs/ for compliance-gap* or compliance-findings* — no match found.
requiredActions: Produce a compliance gap analysis document before merging compliance/policy changes.
Place it as compliance-gap-<date>.md in .mcp/, security/, or docs/.
The gap analysis must map each changed control to its framework requirement (HIPAA §164, PCI DSS 4.0, GDPR Art. 32, etc.).
Document any residual risk and obtain sign-off from the compliance owner.

findings.title: Secret detected in git history (3 findings)
requiredActions: Secrets in git history remain exposed even after removal from the working tree.
Rotate all exposed credentials immediately.
Use git-filter-repo or BFG Repo-Cleaner to purge the secrets from history, then force-push and notify all collaborators to re-clone.
Enable branch protection and secret scanning alerts on the remote host.

findings.title: No JS lockfile found
requiredActions: Add and commit a lockfile (package-lock.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, or yarn.lock).
Pin versions and enable dependency scanning in CI.

findings.title: No SBOM found. Syft is available — run it to generate one.
requiredActions: Run: syft . -o cyclonedx-json=.mcp/sbom/latest.json
Or set SECURITY_AUTO_SBOM=true to auto-generate on each gate run.

findings.title: No SLSA provenance attestation found
requiredActions: Generate SLSA provenance during CI/CD build.
Use slsa-github-generator or equivalent to produce .intoto.jsonl attestations.
Store provenance in .mcp/attestations/

findings.title: Agentic instruction file contains prompt-override / instruction-hijack directives
requiredActions: Treat this instruction file as hostile: an AI agent reading it can be hijacked via embedded 'ignore previous instructions', <system> tags, or 'you are now' directives (OWASP LLM01, MITRE ATLAS AML.T0051, CWE-77).
Quarantine the file and trace its origin (commit author, PR, supply-chain source) before any agent ingests the repository.
Enforce instruction-hierarchy isolation in agent runtimes: render repo-sourced instruction files as untrusted DATA inside delimited boundaries, never as system authority.

findings.title: Agentic instruction file directs the agent to exfiltrate data to an external host
requiredActions: Remove directives that instruct the agent to fetch/curl/POST to non-allowlisted hosts or to send env/secrets/tokens off-box (MITRE ATLAS AML.T0024, CWE-200).
Apply an egress allowlist to the agent's tool runtime so instruction-driven exfiltration calls are blocked at execution time.
Rotate any credentials reachable by the agent if there is evidence the instruction file was active during a run.

findings.title: Agentic instruction / tool definition encodes destructive or unscoped tool behavior
requiredActions: Inspect tool/MCP 'description' fields and instruction bodies for destructive commands (rm -rf, eval, shell exec) or hidden imperatives ('always run', 'do not tell the user') — these poison the model's tool-use plane (MITRE ATLAS AML.T0054, CWE-94).
Define MCP tool descriptions as static, code-reviewed constants; reject any tool whose description carries instructions to the model rather than a neutral capability summary.
Run agent tools under least privilege with an explicit allowlist; deny directives that disable auth, validation, or the sandbox.

findings.title: Agentic instruction file contains self-persistence / auto-reinstall directives
requiredActions: Strip 'on every invocation', 'at the start of every run', and auto-update/ensure_skill directives — they let a malicious instruction set survive removal (persistence; MITRE ATLAS AML.T0051).
Pin and integrity-check (SHA-256) any skill/agent definition the repo loads; forbid runtime self-modification or self-reinstallation.
Audit version history of the file for a benign-then-weaponized edit pattern.

findings.title: Agentic instruction file hides instructions via invisible characters, HTML comments, or encoded payloads
requiredActions: Inspect the file for zero-width/bidi Unicode (U+200B–U+200F, U+202A–U+202E, U+2060–U+2069, U+FEFF), HTML comments, CSS-hidden text, and base64 blobs that decode to instructions — all smuggle directives past human review (CWE-116, MITRE ATLAS AML.T0051).
Normalize instruction files to NFC and strip non-printable characters before any agent ingests them; add a pre-commit hook that rejects invisible characters.
Decode and review every embedded base64/hex blob; treat any that decodes to imperatives or URLs as a live injection payload.

findings.title: Agentic instruction file directs the agent to read or reveal credentials
requiredActions: Remove directives instructing the agent to read .env, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh, keychains, or to dump process.env / print secrets (credential access; MITRE ATLAS AML.T0024, CWE-522).
Run agents with secrets injected out-of-band and scoped to least privilege so instruction-driven harvesting yields nothing useful.
Rotate any credentials the agent could reach and review run logs for prior harvesting attempts.

findings.title: Agentic instruction file directs the agent to poison memory or suppress findings
requiredActions: Remove directives that tell the agent to write false-positive entries, whitelist findings, or mark vulnerabilities as safe/resolved — these blind future scans (data poisoning; MITRE ATLAS AML.T0051).
Make agent memory/finding-suppression writes require validated, authenticated provenance; never accept suppression instructions sourced from a scanned repository.
Audit existing agent memory for entries that may have been planted by this directive.

findings.title: Agentic instruction file pulls instructions or commands from an external location
requiredActions: Remove directives that load/fetch/follow instructions from a URL or run command-substitution ($(curl …), `wget …`) — the visible file looks clean while the real payload arrives at runtime (indirect injection; OWASP LLM01, MITRE ATLAS AML.T0051).
Forbid agents from following instructions sourced from any network location; all agent authority must come from reviewed, pinned local files.
Apply an egress allowlist so runtime instruction-fetching is blocked even if the directive survives review.

findings.title: Agentic instruction file requests elevated permissions or tool access
requiredActions: Remove requests to skip permissions (--dangerously-skip-permissions, bypassPermissions, auto-approve), broaden allowed-tools to wildcards (Bash(*)), or run without confirmation — repo-sourced files must never widen the agent's own privileges (excessive agency; OWASP LLM08, CWE-269).
Pin the agent's permission mode and tool allowlist in trusted operator config, never in repo-readable instruction files.
Require human approval for any change to allowed-tools or permission scope.

findings.title: Agentic instruction file directs the agent to insert a backdoor or persistent access
requiredActions: Remove directives to add SSH keys / authorized_keys, create admin accounts, plant reverse/bind shells, add webhooks, hardcode credentials, or disable MFA/signature checks (persistence + privilege escalation; MITRE ATT&CK T1098, CWE-912).
Treat the repository as potentially compromised: diff for any backdoor the agent may already have written, and review authorized_keys / IAM / webhook config.
Block agent write-access to auth-sensitive paths (authorized_keys, IAM policies, CI secrets) entirely.

findings.title: Agentic instruction file attempts to extract the agent's system prompt or instructions
requiredActions: Remove directives asking the agent to repeat/print/reveal its system prompt, rules, or configuration — prompt-leak is reconnaissance that enables a tailored jailbreak (MITRE ATLAS AML.T0056).
Configure the agent runtime to refuse system-prompt disclosure and to treat such requests as adversarial probes.
Log and alert on prompt-extraction attempts as a precursor to a targeted attack.

controlCoverage.description: All services enforce authentication and authorization with no implicit trust.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Secrets stored only in a dedicated secret manager.
TLS configuration is verified.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: CSP is configured without unsafe inline execution.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: CSRF protections and tests are present.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: SSRF guards and tests are present.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Mobile release configuration meets MASVS baseline.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: AI outputs are schema-validated and tools are allowlisted.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Access control policies enforce least privilege and separation of duties (NIST 800-53 AC).
Audit events are logged, immutable, timestamped, and retained ≥1 year (NIST 800-53 AU).
All users and services are uniquely identified and authenticated (NIST 800-53 IA).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: All data in transit is encrypted using approved algorithms (NIST 800-53 SC).
All inputs are validated, sanitized, and encoded before processing (NIST 800-53 SI).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Incident response playbooks exist, are tested, and have defined MTTD/MTTR SLAs (NIST 800-53 IR).
Cardholder data at rest is encrypted and PAN is never stored in plaintext (PCI DSS 4.0 Req 3).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Cardholder data in transit uses TLS 1.2+ with approved cipher suites (PCI DSS 4.0 Req 4).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Logical access to systems is restricted and uses MFA for privileged accounts (SOC 2 CC6).
System components are monitored for anomalous activity and security events (SOC 2 CC7).
Controls in place to prevent initial access vectors: phishing, supply chain, public-facing exploits (TA0001).
Controls prevent credential dumping, brute force, and credential theft (TA0006).
Controls prevent unauthorized data exfiltration over network, cloud storage, or encoding (TA0010).
Logging is tamper-evident; code signing prevents unauthorized binary substitution (TA0005).
Network segmentation and mTLS prevent lateral movement between services (TA0008).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Data backups are encrypted and tested; ransomware-resistant backup policies in place (TA0040).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Egress filtering blocks C2 channels; DNS monitoring detects beaconing (TA0011).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: LLM outputs are validated against a schema before use in downstream systems (OWASP LLM02).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Rate limiting and input length limits prevent model resource exhaustion (OWASP LLM04).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: LLM plugins and tools operate on an explicit allowlist with minimal permissions (OWASP LLM07).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: LLM agents cannot take irreversible actions without human confirmation (OWASP LLM08).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: ML decision systems affecting people carry fairness/bias evaluation (disparate impact, equalized odds, demographic parity) and representativeness evidence.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Secrets and PII are redacted/tokenized before reaching any LLM payload, preventing shadow-AI data leakage to model providers.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Build artifacts have signed SLSA Level 3 provenance from a hermetic, ephemeral CI build.
Network microsegmentation enforces deny-by-default between all services.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Every request is continuously verified — no persistent trust based on network location.
Business logic abuse scenarios (state skipping, negative values, race conditions) are tested before release.
Proxy and origin servers normalize CL/TE headers; H2C upgrade disabled.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: DNS audit confirms no dangling CNAME records pointing to unprovisioned services.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Multimodal inputs (image, audio, document) are treated as untrusted and cannot inject instructions.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Agent memory and scratchpad content is validated before use in downstream instructions.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Model cannot be probed to recite training PII or memorized secrets.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Mobile release binary uses code obfuscation and anti-instrumentation controls (MASVS-RESILIENCE).
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: EMV 3DS version 2.2+ is used for all card-not-present high-risk transactions.
details: Surface not in scope for this review.

controlCoverage.description: Protected Health Information (PHI) is encrypted at rest and in transit; access is limited and audited.
A dedicated secrets scanner is installed and runnable.
An SBOM generator (Syft) is installed and runnable.
Third-party components are assessed for security risk before adoption (NIST 800-53 SA).
Supply chain integrity is verified via SBOM, provenance attestation, and dependency scanning (NIST 800-53 SR).
All software components are up-to-date and protected against known vulnerabilities (PCI DSS 4.0 Req 6).
A CycloneDX or SPDX SBOM is generated on every release and stored as a signed artifact.
Incident response playbooks exist for all surfaces, are tested within 90 days, and have contact lists.
Only NIST-approved cryptographic algorithms are used (AES-256, SHA-256+, RSA-2048+).
PII, PAN, SSN, and credentials are never written to application logs.

confidence.summary: Automated coverage 4%, scanner readiness 0%, missing controls 23, risk-accepted controls 0. Change type: general.
